title: Packages and public APIs

publication.changeSummary: Language-specific source-linked draft lesson for Packages and public APIs; independent technical, source, pedagogy, accessibility, and runnable-example review remains required. canonicalExplanation.summary: A Python package organizes importable modules under a qualified namespace and exposes a deliberate public API while keeping implementation modules replaceable.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A package re-exports one stable parse function from __init__.py while callers do not depend on its internal _tokens module. Your goal is to create and import a small package with clear internal and public names. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Function contracts and returns; Interpreter and environment; Modules and imports. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Language-specific operational model
sections.body: A Python package organizes importable modules under a qualified namespace and exposes a deliberate public API while keeping implementation modules replaceable. This model is applied through the declared Python: Beginner to Intermediate behavior rather than a language-neutral slogan.

sections.heading: Boundary and failure model
sections.body: Import packages differ from distribution packages, relative imports depend on package context, __init__ execution can create cycles, namespace packages differ, and underscore naming is convention rather than access control. The learner must reject this documented misconception: A directory with Python files is automatically the same thing as an installable distribution and relative imports work from any launch command.

includes: Organize related modules, use relative imports sparingly and expose a deliberate package interface.
Create and import a small package with clear internal and public names.
excludes: Import packages differ from distribution packages, relative imports depend on package context, __init__ execution can create cycles, namespace packages differ, and underscore naming is convention rather than access control.

outcomes: Create and import a small package with clear internal and public names.

examples.title: Packages and public APIs: direct language case
examples.situation: A package re-exports one stable parse function from __init__.py while callers do not depend on its internal _tokens module.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Observe the concrete Python: Beginner to Intermediate state or tool result: A package re-exports one stable parse function from __init__.py while callers do not depend on its internal _tokens module.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the result with this correction: Distinguish import package from distribution package, define a small public namespace, and launch modules with a valid package context.
examples.result: The example meets the atomic outcome because Distinguish import package from distribution package, define a small public namespace, and launch modules with a valid package context.

examples.title: Packages and public APIs: boundary and transfer case
examples.situation: The application launches with python -m package.cli so relative imports have a package context instead of running the nested file directly.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Name what changed from the direct case: The application launches with python -m package.cli so relative imports have a package context instead of running the nested file directly.

Step 2 — Keep the language boundary explicit: Import packages differ from distribution packages, relative imports depend on package context, __init__ execution can create cycles, namespace packages differ, and underscore naming is convention rather than access control.
Step 3 — Reject the tempting but incorrect rule: A directory with Python files is automatically the same thing as an installable distribution and relative imports work from any launch command.
examples.result: This case demonstrates the declared outcome: Create and import a small package with clear internal and public names.

misconceptions.mistakenBelief: A directory with Python files is automatically the same thing as an installable distribution and relative imports work from any launch command.
misconceptions.correction: Distinguish import package from distribution package, define a small public namespace, and launch modules with a valid package context.
misconceptions.diagnosticPrompt: Predict both examples and identify the first observable contradiction in this belief: A directory with Python files is automatically the same thing as an installable distribution and relative imports work from any launch command.

analogy.example: a library whose catalogs, shelves, isolated rooms, and shipping manifest separate import names from installable distributions can introduce Packages and public APIs, after the canonical Python: Beginner to Intermediate rule is stated first.
usefulFor: imports, packages, virtual environments, and pyproject builds
limitations: sys.path, partial module initialization, build backends, editable installs, and wheel contents need direct inspection
